description: Exam 01 Final — Verbal Tenses Question Bank v2 (1st Bimester) — NO 3rd person singular
notes: All questions use I / you / we / they subjects to avoid 3rd person singular (-s, does/doesn't, Does...?), which was not covered in the Verbal Tenses SRS deck. Sentences are original and do not appear in prep_exam_bank.json or exam_01_final_bank.json.

questions.question_text: They _____ in New York.
questions.rationale: Tests Simple Present affirmative with plural subject 'they'. No 3rd person singular -s required.

distractor_rationale.a: lived — Simple Past; no past time marker present
distractor_rationale.d: will live — Simple Future; 'will' requires a future time marker

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: They _____ like coffee.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'don't' auxiliary for Simple Present negative with plural subject 'they'.

distractor_rationale.a: doesn't — 3rd person singular auxiliary (he/she/it), wrong for 'they'
distractor_rationale.b: isn't — auxiliary for Present Continuous, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.c: didn't — Simple Past auxiliary, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: _____ you enjoy swimming?
questions.rationale: Tests the 'Do' auxiliary for Simple Present questions with subject 'you'.

distractor_rationale.a: Are — auxiliary for Present Continuous questions
distractor_rationale.c: Does — 3rd person singular auxiliary, wrong for 'you'
distractor_rationale.d: Did — Simple Past auxiliary, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.card_source: They bought a new car yesterday.
questions.question_text: They _____ a new car yesterday.
questions.rationale: Tests irregular Simple Past form. 'Yesterday' is the canonical Simple Past time marker.

distractor_rationale.b: buys — 3rd person Simple Present, wrong tense and wrong subject
distractor_rationale.d: have bought — Present Perfect; incompatible with specific time marker 'yesterday'

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: I watched a movie last night.
questions.question_text: "I watched a movie last night." — Which tense is used in this sentence?
questions.rationale: Tests recognition of the Simple Past (-ed form) in context. 'Last night' confirms Simple Past.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present: 'I watch a movie' (no -ed, no past marker)
distractor_rationale.b: Present Continuous: 'I am watching a movie' (am + -ing structure)
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect: 'I have watched a movie' — no specific time marker like 'last night'

questions.instruction_pt: Qual tempo verbal é usado nesta frase?

questions.card_source: I am learning to play the guitar.
questions.question_text: I _____ to play the guitar.
questions.rationale: Tests the am + verb-ing structure for Present Continuous with subject 'I'.

distractor_rationale.a: learn — Simple Present base form, used for habits not temporary ongoing actions
distractor_rationale.c: have learned — Present Perfect, implies accumulated experience not current activity
distractor_rationale.d: learned — Simple Past, no past time marker is present

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: We are not (aren't) having dinner yet.
questions.question_text: We _____ having dinner yet.
questions.rationale: Tests the are + not (aren't) structure for Present Continuous negative with 'we'.

distractor_rationale.a: don't — Simple Present negative auxiliary, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.b: didn't — Simple Past negative auxiliary, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.d: haven't — Present Perfect negative auxiliary, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: "Are you listening to me?" — Which tense is this question in?
questions.rationale: Tests recognition of the Are + subject + verb-ing question structure for Present Continuous.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present question: 'Do you listen to me?' (Do + subject + base verb)
distractor_rationale.b: Simple Past question: 'Did you listen to me?' (Did + subject + base verb)
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect question: 'Have you listened to me?' (Have + subject + past participle)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual tempo verbal é usado nesta pergunta?

questions.question_text: They _____ here soon.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'will + base verb' structure for Simple Future. 'Soon' is the key future time marker.

distractor_rationale.a: are — Present Continuous; would need 'are being' or signals current state
distractor_rationale.b: were — Simple Past auxiliary; 'soon' requires future, not past
distractor_rationale.c: have been — Present Perfect; implies completed past action, incompatible with 'soon'

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.tense: Simple Present / Simple Past / Simple Future
questions.question_text: Which time expression matches the Simple Future tense (with 'will')?
questions.rationale: Tests knowledge of time adverbs as tense markers. 'Next week' signals Simple Future, contrasting with past (last night), present ongoing (right now), and habitual present (every morning).

distractor_rationale.a: last night — signals Simple Past (completed past event)
distractor_rationale.b: right now — signals Present Continuous (action in progress)
distractor_rationale.c: every morning — signals Simple Present (habitual action)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual expressão de tempo corresponde ao Simple Future (com 'will')?

questions.card_source: I was reading when the phone rang.
questions.question_text: We _____ when the alarm went off.
questions.rationale: Tests the were + verb-ing structure for Past Continuous. An ongoing background action ('sleeping') is interrupted by a shorter event ('went off').

distractor_rationale.a: slept — Simple Past; treats both actions as sequential, losing the 'in progress' meaning
distractor_rationale.b: have slept — Present Perfect; wrong time frame entirely
distractor_rationale.d: had slept — Past Perfect; implies completion before another past event, not an ongoing action

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: They _____ expecting visitors.
questions.rationale: Tests the were + not (weren't) structure for Past Continuous negative with 'they'.

distractor_rationale.a: didn't — Simple Past negative auxiliary; 'expecting' requires a continuous auxiliary
distractor_rationale.c: haven't — Present Perfect negative; wrong time frame
distractor_rationale.d: don't — Simple Present negative; wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text:
Choose the correct option:

"While we _____ dinner, the power _____ out."
questions.rationale: Tests the Past Continuous / Simple Past contrast: the longer ongoing action uses Past Continuous (were having); the shorter interrupting event uses Simple Past (went).

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses the roles — 'had dinner' (Simple Past) makes dinner a brief sequential event; 'was going' makes the power cut the background action
distractor_rationale.c: Both Past Continuous — loses the interruption relationship; implies both were simultaneous and ongoing
distractor_rationale.d: Both Simple Past — treats both as sequential events, losing the 'while' overlap

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais.

questions.question_text: We _____ the new lab equipment already.
questions.rationale: Tests the have + past participle structure for Present Perfect. 'Already' signals a recently completed action with present relevance.

distractor_rationale.a: test — Simple Present base form, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.b: tested — Simple Past; would require a specific time marker (e.g., 'yesterday'), not 'already'
distractor_rationale.d: were testing — Past Continuous; implies interrupted ongoing past action, not completion

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: _____ they arrived yet?
questions.rationale: Tests the Present Perfect question auxiliary 'Have'. 'Yet' is a classic Present Perfect question marker.

distractor_rationale.a: Did — Simple Past auxiliary; would need base verb 'arrive', not past participle 'arrived'

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.question_text:
Choose the correct tense for each blank:

"We _____ the city (a) LAST SUMMER. We _____ it (b) THREE TIMES."
questions.rationale: Tests the core PP vs Simple Past distinction: 'last summer' (specific past time) requires Simple Past; 'three times' (unspecified accumulated experience) requires Present Perfect.

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses the rule — 'have visited last summer' is ungrammatical; Present Perfect rejects specific past time markers
distractor_rationale.c: All Simple Past — misses the Present Perfect for unspecified experience
distractor_rationale.d: All Present Perfect — 'have visited last summer' is ungrammatical

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais para as duas frases.

questions.card_source: They are building a new house this year.
questions.question_text: We _____ start the project next month.
questions.rationale: Tests the are + going to + base verb structure for planned future actions with plural subject 'we'.

distractor_rationale.a: 'will going to' — double future marker; ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.c: 'go to' — Simple Present; 'next month' signals future not habitual present
distractor_rationale.d: 'going to' alone — missing the auxiliary 'are'; grammatically incomplete

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: They are not going to use that method anymore.
questions.question_text: We _____ accept these conditions.
questions.rationale: Tests the are + not + going to structure for negating a future plan. Plural subject 'we' requires 'aren't going to'.

distractor_rationale.a: 'won't going to' — mixes 'will' negative with 'going to'; ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.b: 'don't going to' — Simple Present auxiliary with 'going to'; ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.d: 'haven't going to' — Present Perfect auxiliary with 'going to'; ungrammatical

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text:
You answer the phone and hear a friend say: "I need help with my homework." You reply: "OK, _____ help you."

Which option correctly completes your reply?
questions.rationale: Tests the will vs going to distinction. 'Will' is used for spontaneous decisions made at the moment of speaking. 'Going to' is for pre-planned intentions — you hadn't planned to help before the call.

distractor_rationale.a: 'I am going to' — implies a pre-existing plan; you only decided to help after hearing the request
distractor_rationale.c: 'I am' alone — grammatically incomplete as a future statement
distractor_rationale.d: 'I have' — Present Perfect auxiliary; doesn't express a future intention

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a forma correta para expressar uma decisão espontânea.

questions.question_text:
Read the passage:

"We usually meet on Fridays. Last Friday, we discussed the new rules. Right now, we are writing the final report. We have already sent the first draft."

Which sentence uses the Present Continuous?

options.text: We are writing the final report.
We have already sent the first draft.

questions.rationale: Tests tense recognition across four tenses: Simple Present ('usually'), Simple Past ('Last Friday'), Present Continuous ('Right now' + are + -ing), and Present Perfect ('already' + have + past participle).
questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e identifique qual frase usa o Present Continuous.
